text:
## ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS  
### CNG Guidelines

**Mission Statement:**  
The mission of the Comanche Nation Environmental Programs is to safeguard, preserve, and sustainably manage the Nation's natural resources for current and future generations.

---

**Service Area:**  
Most services are provided in Comanche, Cotton, Tillman, Kiowa, Caddo, Stephens, Jackson, and Grady.  
There is no defined service area, but services are based off resource availability and tribal member’s location.  
If you have questions, please contact our office at 580-492-3754.

---

**Eligibility Requirement:**  
Comanche Nation Tribal Member and Proof of Address (or TSR, in case of illegal dumpsite clean-up)

---

**Services:**  
Applications: online, in office, can be emailed, mailed, or over the phone (elders).

---

### Integrated Pest Management
- Bed Bug Treatment – Once a year  

---

### Solid Waste
- Illegal Dumpsite Clean Ups – One-time service only  
- 20-yard roll offs, dump trailers – Once a year  

---

### Indoor Air Quality
- Air Duct Cleanings – Every 3–5 years  
- Indoor Mold Testing and Analysis – Pre and Posting with follow up testing as needed  

---

### Water
- Drinking Water Panel – Testing once a year or as needed  
- Well Water Testing – Testing once a year or as needed  

---

### Recycling
- Our facilities are open M–F 7:30 am – 6:00 pm  
  - Car batteries  
  - Paint  
  - Plastic  
  - Cardboard  
  - Aluminum  
  - Glass  
  - Tires  
  - Scrap Metal  
  - Oils  
  - Electronic Waste (everything except T.V.'s) 
  - Appliances 
  -Furniture

## CNEP Application Process

Once our office receives your complete application, we will contact you to schedule an assessment. All services come with assessments and follow-up analysis results.

Assessments are important for a successful service by clarifying needs, managing expectations, and ensuring efficient and effective service delivery.

- An assessment allows CNEP to gather detailed information about the client's needs, goals, and expectations. This ensures that the service CNEP provides is tailored to address their specific requirements.  
- By conducting an assessment, CNEP can identify any underlying issues, challenges, or risks that may affect delivery service. This enables you to proactively address problems before they arise.  
- Each client may have unique circumstances or preferences. An assessment helps CNEP to design a personalized plan or approach that is most effective for clients.  
- An assessment provides a foundation for ongoing communication between our program and the client. It ensures both parties are aligned in terms of expectations and goals.  
- After the assessment is completed, the client will sign that it has been completed.  
- Once your assessment is complete, you will be placed in the queue for service. CNEP processes applications based on the order in which they are received. This system ensures fairness for all clients submitting applications.  
- When it is your turn to receive service, you will receive a courtesy call as a reminder of the agreed-upon date and time. If that time no longer works for you, you will have the opportunity to reschedule. On the day of service, our office will give you another courtesy call prior to performing the service. During this call, we will inform you if we are running ahead or behind schedule and provide an estimated arrival time.  
- The duration of the service depends on the type of service being provided. Please refer to the breakdown of each service for more details.  
- After the service is completed, you will be asked to sign to confirm that the service was performed. You will also be encouraged to complete a brief survey about your experience with our services.  

---

## Renewal Process

**Renewal Application:** Once your service is complete, your application will be closed. You may reapply for our services once a year. Please note that water and mold testing may require follow-up tests. Depending on the severity of the issue, additional tests may be necessary, and we will discuss this with you.

If you have previously applied, please notify our office so we can locate your previous application. When reapplying for service, you will only need to update your application. There is no need to resubmit your
